extra.reasoning:
vision_loss appears in exactly one rule, interaction_drop (priority 100), which sets acuity to self_care (lowest) when focal_weakness AND leg_swelling AND back_pain AND vision_loss are all true. Because priority 100 dominates every other rule, turning vision_loss on can collapse a high acuity down to self_care.

Counterexample baseline: focal_weakness=true, leg_swelling=true, back_pain=true, dyspnea=false, vision_loss=false. Fired rules: raise_leg_swelling (prio 3 -> emergent), raise_back_pain (prio 2 -> urgent), raise_focal_weakness (prio 2 -> urgent). Highest priority wins -> emergent.

Now set vision_loss=true (all else unchanged): interaction_drop (prio 100) fires -> self_care, dominating all lower-priority rules -> acuity = self_care.

Acuity drops emergent -> self_care when vision_loss is set true, which violates monotonicity (setting vision_loss true must never lower acuity). The witness_assignment is the vision_loss=true input that yields the lowered acuity.
